title: Async Task with Status Polling
description: Write a playbook that launches a long-running database migration asynchronously and polls for completion using async_status.
starterCode:
---
# Write a playbook that:
# 1. Uses 'strategy: free' for parallel execution
# 2. Runs '/opt/db/migrate.sh' with async: 1800 and poll: 0
#    Register the result as 'migration_job'
# 3. Uses ansible.builtin.async_status with the job ID
#    to poll every 30 seconds, up to 60 retries
# 4. Uses 'until: job_result.finished' to detect completion

- name: Database migration
  hosts: dbservers
  become: true
  # Add strategy here
  tasks:
    - name: Start database migration
      # async + poll: 0 for fire-and-forget

    - name: Wait for migration completion
      # Use ansible.builtin.async_status
solutionCode:
---
- name: Database migration
  hosts: dbservers
  become: true
  strategy: free
  tasks:
    - name: Start database migration
      ansible.builtin.command: /opt/db/migrate.sh
      async: 1800
      poll: 0
      register: migration_job

    - name: Wait for migration completion
      ansible.builtin.async_status:
        jid: "{{ migration_job.ansible_job_id }}"
      register: job_result
      until: job_result.finished
      retries: 60
      delay: 30
hints: async: N sets the maximum runtime in seconds
poll: 0 means 'fire and forget' — don't wait for it
async_status needs the 'jid' (job ID) from the registered variable
Use until: job_result.finished with retries and delay to poll

validationRules.errorMessage: Add 'strategy: free' for parallel host execution
Add 'async:' to run the task asynchronously
Use ansible.builtin.async_status to check job status

simulatedOutput.success:
PLAY [Database migration] ******************************

TASK [Start database migration] ************************
changed: [db1.example.com]
changed: [db2.example.com]

TASK [Wait for migration completion] *******************
RETRY [Wait for migration completion](1): 2 of 60
RETRY [Wait for migration completion](2): 3 of 60
ok: [db1.example.com]
ok: [db2.example.com]

PLAY RECAP *********************************************
db1.example.com : ok=2  changed=1  unreachable=0  failed=0
db2.example.com : ok=2  changed=1  unreachable=0  failed=0
simulatedOutput.failure: ERROR! Check async:, poll: 0, async_status module, and until: job_result.finished.
